patterns.name: Short A (CVC)
patterns.rule: When 'a' is between two consonants, it usually makes the short /æ/ sound

patterns.name: Short E (CVC)
patterns.rule: When 'e' is between two consonants, it usually makes the short /ɛ/ sound

patterns.name: Short I (CVC)
patterns.rule: When 'i' is between two consonants, it usually makes the short /ɪ/ sound

patterns.name: Magic E (Silent E)
patterns.description: Adding 'e' at the end makes the vowel say its name
patterns.rule: When a word ends in consonant + e, the vowel before the consonant says its long sound (its name)
patterns.tip: Magic E jumps over one consonant to make the vowel say its name!

patterns.name: Consonant Blends
patterns.description: Two or three consonants blended together at the beginning or end
patterns.rule: When consonants are next to each other, blend their sounds together smoothly
patterns.tip: Say both consonant sounds quickly together

patterns.name: Double Consonants
patterns.rule: Double consonants often appear after a short vowel sound in the middle of a word
patterns.tip: After a short vowel, double the consonant before adding -ing or -ed

patterns.name: Silent Letters
patterns.description: Words with letters that are written but not pronounced
patterns.rule: Some letters in English words are silent - you write them but don't say them
patterns.tip: Silent 'k' before 'n' (know), silent 'w' before 'r' (write), silent 'b' after 'm' (climb)

patterns.name: I before E
patterns.description: The famous 'i before e except after c' rule
patterns.rule: Usually 'i' comes before 'e' (believe), except after 'c' where 'e' comes before 'i' (receive)
patterns.tip: I before E, except after C, or when sounding like A as in neighbor and weigh

patterns.name: Prefix un-
patterns.description: Adding 'un-' to the beginning of a word to mean 'not'
patterns.rule: The prefix 'un-' means 'not' or 'opposite of'. Just add it to the beginning without changing the base word
patterns.tip: un + happy = unhappy. The base word stays the same!

patterns.name: Suffix -tion
patterns.rule: The suffix '-tion' is pronounced 'shun' and turns verbs into nouns
patterns.tip: -tion says 'shun'. Use -tion after most consonants and long vowels
